picks.title: Ed van der Elsken
picks.blurb: New photography exhibition opening this weekend: iconic 20th-century Dutch photographer with evocative street scenes.
picks.why: Freshly opened, final month on display

picks.title: Master of Impressionism (closing)
picks.blurb: Immersive digital art finale: Monet's masterpieces projected across walls in hypnotic motion.

picks.title: Tata Ronkholz & Yumna Al-Arashi (closing)
picks.blurb: Final weekend: German photographer's urban visions & Arab world perspectives by Yemeni-Egyptian-American artist.
picks.why: Last chance to see these exhibitions

picks.title: deLux: Ydro
picks.blurb: Escape's Sunday night special with Ydro headlining rotation lineup of house and techno DJs.

picks.title: Klankenfestijn (Sound Festival) + Mooie Dingen Markt
picks.blurb: Live music festival paired with beautiful things market in the park. Browse crafts, food, and enjoy outdoor performances.
picks.why: Free outdoor festival with live music and shopping

picks.title: Solstice Festival
picks.blurb: Psychedelic music festival celebrating summer solstice with DJs, live performances, ritual and dance on longest day of year.
picks.why: Midsummer celebration with afterparty until sunrise

picks.title: Jardin Rouge Festival
picks.blurb: Multi-day festival spanning into the weekend with diverse music programming across three days.

picks.title: Freestyle Rap Workshop
picks.blurb: High-energy beginner-friendly workshop on freestyle fundamentals with NYC-based rapper David Bradshaw.
picks.why: Led by international freestyle rap educator

picks.title: Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.blurb: Summer theatre series. Dance performances Fri night, then comedy, music, cabaret all season. Bring a picnic.
picks.why: Nightly free performances in city's most beloved park

picks.title: Port Festival & Zeehavendagen (Seaport Days)
picks.blurb: Free festival with dozens of ships to tour, demo boat rides, maritime activities, and energy-focused installations for all ages.
picks.why: Largest family-friendly maritime event, free access

picks.title: Artis Summer Nights & Hortus Botanicus Music on Sunday
picks.blurb: Zoo stays open until dusk on Saturdays in June with live music, fairy lights, and nocturnal animal displays. Hortus hosts evening concerts with standard admissi

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists reinterpret Eye's colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname in ten new commissioned works.

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Iconic exhibition of the year's most compelling photojournalism, running through September at Amsterdam's iconic church.

picks.title: LuminAir Rooftop Bar
picks.when: Open daily; Father's Day specials Sat 20–Sun 21 Jun
picks.blurb: Elevated sky bar 100m+ above canals. Panoramic city views, signature cocktails, complimentary whisky for dads this weekend.
picks.why: Father's Day specials, iconic rooftop location

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Major food festival showcasing Amsterdam's best chefs, street food, and culinary talent across three days.
picks.why: City's premier food event; unmissable for foodies
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam, Bite of Amsterdam official · Bite of Amsterdam official & I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Your Little Black Book · I amsterdam

picks.title: Bacchus Wine Festival
picks.blurb: Taste 300+ wines from around the world paired with dishes and DJ music. Part of three-weekend festival series (also Jun 5–7, 12–14).
picks.source: I amsterdam, Amsterdam official · Bacchus Wijnfestival · I amsterdam · AmsterdamSights & I amsterdam · Amsterdam Spotted

picks.title: Holland Festival (various venues)
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's premier performing arts festival: theatre, dance, opera, music at Carré, Concertgebouw, Muziekgebouw, and beyond.
picks.source: Songkick · I amsterdam · Songkick / Het Concertgebouw · Holland Festival

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.venue: Private gardens throughout Amsterdam (canal houses, institut
picks.blurb: Explore 30+ hidden private canal gardens, courtyards, and roof terraces across historic Amsterdam houses, guided by Museum van Loon.
picks.why: Annual event, unique access to secret gardens
picks.source: I amsterdam, Amsterdam official · I amsterdam · I amsterdam Culture Guide

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: District-wide free activities including street art tours, brewery tastings, DJ sets, bird-watching, workshops and music across spaces.
picks.why: Free weekend full of diverse cultural activities
picks.source: I amsterdam weekend guide · I amsterdam

picks.title: Bloodywood
picks.blurb: Indian folk-metal fusion with dhol drums, Hindi/Punjabi rap. Latest album 'Nu Delhi' live.
picks.why: Bold cross-cultural metal sound, rarely seen in Amsterdam

picks.title: John Summit
picks.blurb: Dance powerhouse John Summit: tech house, techno, drum & bass. European headline tour stop.
picks.why: Sold out, Lollapalooza headliner, boundary-pushing producer

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Sunday market with arts, crafts, local artisan goods, food stalls, music and entertainment for families.
picks.why: Free entry, arts and food in iconic square

picks.title: Mystic Garden – Echo's of Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Local techno, house, trance festival reimagined across park stages. Four Amsterdam collectives, interactive art installations.
picks.why: Major Sat-only festival, local artists, brand new concept
picks.source: Mystic Garden Festival / DJ Guide · DJ Guide

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: GRIMM (8+) — Hiphop-Ballet Fairytale Mix
picks.blurb: Fairytales reimagined with explosive hiphop, ballet, and dark humour. Sprightly, dynamic, and visually stunning for families.
picks.why: Spectacular fusion of dance styles; critics & families love

picks.title: Jodeci
picks.blurb: R&B legends bring '90s nostalgia to AFAS Live for an intimate Amsterdam date.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Escape Club: deLux Ydro
picks.blurb: Sunday night house session with rotating DJs including Irwan, Kimberlee Ramirez, Jay Junior, Fella, Della, and Casanova.

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026 — Free Family Activities at Java Island
picks.blurb: Waterfront celebration with free harbour activities, demonstrations, and entertainment for all ages.
picks.why: Free, outdoors, diverse programme for the whole family

picks.title: Bacchus Wijnfestival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's oldest wine festival: 300+ wines, live music, snacks & stories. Free for children.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses and Mammoth
picks.blurb: Rock legends headline Amsterdam's largest indoor venue with special guest Mammoth WVH.
picks.why: Major headliner, likely to sell out

picks.title: Finale Koninklijk Concertgebouw Concours 2026
picks.blurb: Six mini-concerts from young classical musicians competing in this prestigious competition final. Inspiring and accessible.
picks.why: Thrilling live music finale; young talent showcase

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: FC Hyena
picks.blurb: An arthouse cinema with a wine bar and a small kitchen, right on the IJ in Noord.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen, restored prints in 35mm whenever possible. Eye's ongoing classics programming.

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
